name: Remote Terminal (Go Agent + TUI Client)
description: Go-based remote terminal with both a TUI client and an agent, connected via NoLag

topics.name: commands
topics.description: Commands sent from the client to the agent (shell, session_start, session_input, etc.)

topics.name: responses
topics.description: Command responses returned from the agent to the client

topics.name: status
topics.description: Device status heartbeats broadcast by the agent every 30 seconds

topics.name: session_output
topics.description: Interactive PTY session output streamed from the agent to the client

readme:
# Remote Terminal (Go Agent + TUI Client)

## Description
A Go-based remote terminal system with two binaries: an **agent** that runs on the target machine and a **TUI client** (Bubbletea) for connecting from your local terminal. Supports shell commands, interactive PTY sessions, file transfer, tab completion, device discovery, and command history.

This blueprint includes both the agent and a terminal-based client. For a browser-based client, use:
- [remote-terminal-web](../remote-terminal-web/) -- Browser-based terminal client

Alternative agents are available in other languages:
- [remote-terminal-node](../remote-terminal-node/) -- Node.js agent
- [remote-terminal-python](../remote-terminal-python/) -- Python agent

## Getting Started

### Agent (run on target machine)
1. Download or clone this blueprint to the target machine
2. Build: `go build -o agent ./cmd/agent`
3. Run:
   ```
   ./agent -token <YOUR_TOKEN> -apikey <YOUR_API_KEY> -appid <YOUR_APP_ID>
   ```

### TUI Client (run locally)
1. Build: `go build -o client ./cmd/client`
2. Run:
   ```
   ./client -token <YOUR_TOKEN> -device <DEVICE_ID>
   ```
3. Use `!help` for available commands, arrow keys for history, Tab for auto-complete

## Architecture
- **Agent** (`cmd/agent/`): subscribes to `commands`, executes locally, streams output back
- **TUI Client** (`cmd/client/`): Bubbletea-based terminal UI with viewport scrolling, command history, and tab completion
- Each device gets its own room (room slug = device ID)
- Agent auto-creates its room via the NoLag REST API on startup
- Device discovery via `status` topic heartbeats

## NoLag Features Used
- **Pub/Sub messaging** for command/response exchange
- **Room-per-device** with dynamic room creation via REST API
- **Reconnection** for resilient long-running agent connections
- **Binary-safe messaging** via base64 encoding for PTY data
- **Go SDK** (`github.com/NoLagApp/nolag-go`) for both agent and client
